methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase._compute_main_properties
documentation: ## Benchmarking methods

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_phi_consistency
documentation:
Method to calculate and return a consistency check between ideal
gas entropy behavior, and the fugacity coefficients and their
temperature derivatives.

$$
S = S^{ig} - \sum_{i} z_i R\left(\ln \phi_i +  T \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}\right)
$$

# Returns

error

: Relative consistency error
  $|1 - S^{\text{from phi}}/S^\text{implemented}|$, \[-\]
## Consistency Checks

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_phi_consistency
documentation:
Method to calculate and return a consistency check between ideal
gas enthalpy behavior, and the fugacity coefficients and their
temperature derivatives.

$$
H^{\text{from phi}} = H^{ig} - RT^2\sum_i z_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}
$$

# Returns

error

: Relative consistency error
  $|1 - H^{\text{from phi}}/H^\text{implemented}|$, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_dep_phi_consistency
documentation:
Method to calculate and return a consistency check between
departure Gibbs free energy, and the fugacity coefficients.

$$
G^{\text{from phi}}_{dep} = RT\sum_i z_i \phi_i
$$

# Returns

error

: Relative consistency error
  $|1 - G^{\text{from phi}}_{dep}/G^\text{implemented}_{dep}|$, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_dep_phi_consistency
documentation:
Method to calculate and return a consistency check between
departure enthalpy, and the fugacity coefficients' temperature
derivatives.

$$
H^{\text{from phi}}_{dep} = -RT^2\sum_i z_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}
$$

# Returns

error

: Relative consistency error
  $|1 - H^{\text{from phi}}_{dep}/H^\text{implemented}_{dep}|$, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_dep_phi_consistency
documentation:
Method to calculate and return a consistency check between ideal
gas entropy behavior, and the fugacity coefficients and their
temperature derivatives.

$$
S_{dep}^{\text{from phi}} = - \sum_{i} z_i R\left(\ln \phi_i +  T \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}\right)
$$

# Returns

error

: Relative consistency error
  $|1 - S^{\text{from phi}}_{dep}/S^\text{implemented}_{dep}|$, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.V_phi_consistency
documentation:
Method to calculate and return a consistency check between
molar volume, and the fugacity coefficients' pressures
derivatives.

$$
V^{\text{from phi P der}} = \left(\left(\sum_i z_i \frac{\partial \ln  \phi_i}{\partial P}\right)P + 1\right)RT/P
$$

# Returns

error

: Relative consistency error
  $|1 - V^{\text{from phi P der}}/V^\text{implemented}|$, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_from_phi
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the enthalpy of the fluid as
calculated from the ideal-gas enthalpy and the the fugacity
coefficients' temperature derivatives.

$$
H^{\text{from phi}} = H^{ig} - RT^2\sum_i z_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}
$$

# Returns

H

: Enthalpy as calculated from fugacity coefficient temperature
  derivatives \[J/mol\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_from_phi
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the entropy of the fluid as
calculated from the ideal-gas entropy and the the fugacity
coefficients' temperature derivatives.

$$
S = S^{ig} - \sum_{i} z_i R\left(\ln \phi_i +  T \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}\right)
$$

# Returns

S

: Entropy as calculated from fugacity coefficient temperature
  derivatives \[J/(mol\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.V_from_phi
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar volume of the fluid as
calculated from the pressure derivatives of fugacity coefficients.

$$
V^{\text{from phi P der}} = \left(\left(\sum_i z_i \frac{\partial \ln  \phi_i}{\partial P}\right)P + 1\right)RT/P
$$

# Returns

V

: Molar volume, \[m^3/mol\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_min_criteria
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Gibbs energy criteria required
for comparing phase stability. This calculation can be faster
than calculating the full Gibbs energy. For this comparison to work,
all phases must use the ideal gas basis.

$$
G^{\text{criteria}} = G^{dep} + RT\sum_i z_i \ln z_i
$$

# Returns

G_crit

: Gibbs free energy like criteria \[J/mol\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.lnphis_at_zs
documentation:
Method to directly calculate the log fugacity coefficients at a
different composition than the current phase.
This is implemented to allow for the possibility of more direct
calls to obtain fugacities than is possible with the phase interface.
This base method simply creates a new phase, gets its log fugacity
coefficients, and returns them.

Returns
-------
lnphis : list[float]
    Log fugacity coefficients, [-]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.fugacities_at_zs
documentation:
Method to directly calculate the figacities at a
different composition than the current phase.
This is implemented to allow for the possibility of more direct
calls to obtain fugacities than is possible with the phase interface.
This base method simply creates a new phase, gets its log fugacity
coefficients, exponentiates them, and multiplies them by `P` and
compositions.

Returns
-------
fugacities : list[float]
    Fugacities, [Pa]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.lnphi
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the log of fugacity coefficient of
the phase; provided the phase is 1 component.

Returns
-------
lnphi : list[float]
    Log fugacity coefficient, [-]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.phi
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the fugacity coefficient of
the phase; provided the phase is 1 component.

Returns
-------
phi : list[float]
    Fugacity coefficient, [-]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.fugacity
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the fugacity of
the phase; provided the phase is 1 component.

Returns
-------
fugacity : list[float]
    Fugacity, [Pa]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dfugacity_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of
fugacity of the phase; provided the phase is 1 component.

Returns
-------
dfugacity_dT : list[float]
    Fugacity first temperature derivative, [Pa/K]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dfugacity_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative of
fugacity of the phase; provided the phase is 1 component.

Returns
-------
dfugacity_dP : list[float]
    Fugacity first pressure derivative, [-]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.fugacities
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the fugacities of the phase.

$$
f_i = P z_i \exp(\ln \phi_i)
$$

# Returns

fugacities

: Fugacities, \[Pa\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.lnfugacities
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the log of fugacities of the phase.

$$
\ln f_i = \ln\left( P z_i \exp(\ln \phi_i)\right) = \ln(P) + \ln(z_i) + \ln \phi_i
$$

# Returns

lnfugacities

: Log fugacities, \[log(Pa)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dfugacities_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of fugacities
of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial f_i}{\partial T} = P z_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}
$$

# Returns

dfugacities_dT

: Temperature derivative of fugacities of all components
  in the phase, \[Pa/K\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.lnphis_G_min
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the log fugacity coefficients of the
phase. If the phase can have multiple solutions at its `T` and `P`,
this method should return those with the lowest Gibbs energy. This
needs to be implemented on phases with that criteria like cubic EOSs.

Returns
-------
lnphis : list[float]
    Log fugacity coefficients, [-]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.phis
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the fugacity coefficients of the
phase.

$$
\phi_i = \exp (\ln \phi_i)
$$

# Returns

phis

: Fugacity coefficients, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dphis_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of fugacity
coefficients of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \phi_i}{\partial T} = \phi_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial T}
$$

# Returns

dphis_dT

: Temperature derivative of fugacity coefficients of all components
  in the phase, \[1/K\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dphis_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative of fugacity
coefficients of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \phi_i}{\partial P} = \phi_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial P}
$$

# Returns

dphis_dP

: Pressure derivative of fugacity coefficients of all components
  in the phase, \[1/Pa\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dphis_dzs
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar composition derivative of
fugacity coefficients of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \phi_i}{\partial z_j} = \phi_i \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial z_j}
$$

# Returns

dphis_dzs

: Molar derivative of fugacity coefficients of all components
  in the phase, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dfugacities_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative of the
fugacities of the components in the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial f_i}{\partial P} = z_i \left(P \frac{\partial \phi_i}{\partial P}  + \phi_i \right)
$$

# Returns

dfugacities_dP

: Pressure derivative of fugacities of all components
  in the phase, \[-\]

# Notes

For models without pressure dependence of fugacity, the returned result
may not be exactly zero due to inaccuracy in floating point results;
results are likely on the order of 1e-14 or lower in that case.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dfugacities_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole number derivative of the
fugacities of the components in the phase.

if i != j:

$$
\frac{\partial f_i}{\partial n_j} = P\phi_i z_i \left( \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial n_j} - 1 \right)
$$

if i == j:

$$
\frac{\partial f_i}{\partial n_j} = P\phi_i z_i \left( \frac{\partial \ln \phi_i}{\partial n_j} - 1 \right) + P\phi_i
$$

# Returns

dfugacities_dns

: Mole number derivatives of the fugacities of all components
  in the phase, \[Pa/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dlnfugacities_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole number derivative of the
log of fugacities of the components in the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \ln f_i}{\partial n_j} = \frac{1}{f_i} \frac{\partial  f_i}{\partial n_j}
$$

# Returns

dlnfugacities_dns

: Mole number derivatives of the log of fugacities of all components
  in the phase, \[log(Pa)/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dlnfugacities_dzs
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole fraction derivative of the
log of fugacities of the components in the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \ln f_i}{\partial z_j} = \frac{1}{f_i} \frac{\partial  f_i}{\partial z_j}
$$

# Returns

dlnfugacities_dzs

: Mole fraction derivatives of the log of fugacities of all components
  in the phase, \[log(Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.log_zs
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the log of mole fractions specified.
These are used in calculating entropy and in many other formulas.

$$
\ln z_i
$$

# Returns

log_zs

: Log of mole fractions, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.V_iter
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the volume of the phase in a way
suitable for a TV resolution to converge on the same pressure. This
often means the return value of this method is an mpmath `mpf`.
This dummy method simply returns the implemented V method.

Returns
-------
V : float or mpf
    Molar volume, [m^3/mol]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Gibbs free energy of the phase.

$$
G = H - TS
$$

# Returns

G

: Gibbs free energy, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the internal energy of the phase.

$$
U = H - PV
$$

# Returns

U

: Internal energy, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Helmholtz energy of the phase.

$$
A = U - TS
$$

# Returns

A

: Helmholtz energy, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dH_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole number derivative of the
enthalpy of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial H}{\partial n_i}
$$

# Returns

dH_dns

: Mole number derivatives of the enthalpy of the phase,
  \[J/mol^2\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dS_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole number derivative of the
entropy of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial S}{\partial n_i}
$$

# Returns

dS_dns

: Mole number derivatives of the entropy of the phase,
  \[J/(mol^2\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dG_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure
temperature derivative of Gibbs free energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial T}\right)_{P} = -T\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_{P} - S + \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial T}\right)_{P}
$$

# Returns

dG_dT

: Constant-pressure temperature derivative of Gibbs free energy,
  \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dG_dT_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume
temperature derivative of Gibbs free energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial T}\right)_{V} = -T\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_{V} - S + \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial T}\right)_{V}
$$

# Returns

dG_dT_V

: Constant-volume temperature derivative of Gibbs free energy,
  \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dG_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature
pressure derivative of Gibbs free energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial P}\right)_{T} = -T\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial P}\right)_{T} + \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial P}\right)_{T}
$$

# Returns

dG_dP

: Constant-temperature pressure derivative of Gibbs free energy,
  \[J/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dG_dP_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume
pressure derivative of Gibbs free energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial P}\right)_{V} = -T\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial P}\right)_{V} - S \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial P}\right)_{V} + \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial P}\right)_{V}
$$

# Returns

dG_dP_V

: Constant-volume pressure derivative of Gibbs free energy,
  \[J/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dG_dV_T
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature
volume derivative of Gibbs free energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial V}\right)_{T} = \left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial P}\right)_{T} \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_{T}
$$

# Returns

dG_dV_T

: Constant-temperature volume derivative of Gibbs free energy,
  \[J/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dG_dV_P
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure
volume derivative of Gibbs free energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial V}\right)_{P} = \left(\frac{\partial G}{\partial T}\right)_{P} \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial V}\right)_{P}
$$

# Returns

dG_dV_P

: Constant-pressure volume derivative of Gibbs free energy,
  \[J/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dU_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure
temperature derivative of internal energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial T}\right)_{P} = -P \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_{P}  + \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial T}\right)_{P}
$$

# Returns

dU_dT

: Constant-pressure temperature derivative of internal energy,
  \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dU_dT_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume
temperature derivative of internal energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial T}\right)_{V} = \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial T}\right)_{V}  - V \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_{V}
$$

# Returns

dU_dT_V

: Constant-volume temperature derivative of internal energy,
  \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dU_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature
pressure derivative of internal energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial P}\right)_{T} = -P \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P}\right)_{T}  - V + \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial P}\right)_{T}
$$

# Returns

dU_dP

: Constant-temperature pressure derivative of internal energy,
  \[J/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dU_dP_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume
pressure derivative of internal energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial P}\right)_{V} = \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial P}\right)_{V}  - V
$$

# Returns

dU_dP_V

: Constant-volume pressure derivative of internal energy,
  \[J/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dU_dV_T
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature
volume derivative of internal energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial V}\right)_{T} = \left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial P}\right)_{T}  \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_{T}
$$

# Returns

dU_dV_T

: Constant-temperature volume derivative of internal energy,
  \[J/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dU_dV_P
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure
volume derivative of internal energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial V}\right)_{P} = \left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial T}\right)_{P}  \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial V}\right)_{P}
$$

# Returns

dU_dV_P

: Constant-pressure volume derivative of internal energy,
  \[J/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dA_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure
temperature derivative of Helmholtz energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial T}\right)_{P} = -T \left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_{P}  - S + \left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial T}\right)_{P}
$$

# Returns

dA_dT

: Constant-pressure temperature derivative of Helmholtz energy,
  \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dA_dT_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume
temperature derivative of Helmholtz energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial T}\right)_{V} =  \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial T}\right)_{V}  - V \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_{V}  - T \left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_{V}  - S
$$

# Returns

dA_dT_V

: Constant-volume temperature derivative of Helmholtz energy,
  \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dA_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature
pressure derivative of Helmholtz energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial P}\right)_{T} = -T \left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial P}\right)_{T}  + \left(\frac{\partial U}{\partial P}\right)_{T}
$$

# Returns

dA_dP

: Constant-temperature pressure derivative of Helmholtz energy,
  \[J/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dA_dP_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume
pressure derivative of Helmholtz energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial P}\right)_{V} = \left(\frac{\partial H}{\partial P}\right)_{V} - V - S\left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial P}\right)_{V} -T \left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial P}\right)_{V}
$$

# Returns

dA_dP_V

: Constant-volume pressure derivative of Helmholtz energy,
  \[J/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dA_dV_T
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature
volume derivative of Helmholtz energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial V}\right)_{T} = \left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial P}\right)_{T}   \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_{T}
$$

# Returns

dA_dV_T

: Constant-temperature volume derivative of Helmholtz energy,
  \[J/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dA_dV_P
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure
volume derivative of Helmholtz energy.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial V}\right)_{P} = \left(\frac{\partial A}{\partial T}\right)_{P}   \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial V}\right)_{P}
$$

# Returns

dA_dV_P

: Constant-pressure volume derivative of Helmholtz energy,
  \[J/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_dep
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the departure Gibbs free energy of
the phase.

$$
G_{dep} = H_{dep} - TS_{dep}
$$

# Returns

G_dep

: Departure Gibbs free energy, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.V_dep
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the departure (from ideal gas
behavior) molar volume of the phase.

$$
V_{dep} = V - \frac{RT}{P}
$$

# Returns

V_dep

: Departure molar volume, \[m^3/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U_dep
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the departure internal energy of
the phase.

$$
U_{dep} = H_{dep} - PV_{dep}
$$

# Returns

U_dep

: Departure internal energy, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A_dep
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the departure Helmholtz energy of
the phase.

$$
A_{dep} = U_{dep} - TS_{dep}
$$

# Returns

A_dep

: Departure Helmholtz energy, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_dep_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass departure enthalpy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
H_dep_mass : float
    Departure mass enthalpy free energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_dep_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass departure entropy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
S_dep_mass : float
    Departure mass entropy free energy, [J/(kg*K)]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_dep_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass departure Gibbs free energy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
G_dep_mass : float
    Departure mass Gibbs free energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U_dep_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the departure mass internal energy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
U_dep_mass : float
    Departure mass internal energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A_dep_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the departure mass Helmholtz energy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
A_dep_mass : float
    Departure mass Helmholtz energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_reactive
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the enthalpy of the phase on a
reactive basis, using the `Hfs` values of the phase.

$$
H_{reactive} = H + \sum_i z_i {H_{f,i}}
$$

# Returns

H_reactive

: Enthalpy of the phase on a reactive basis, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_reactive
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the entropy of the phase on a
reactive basis, using the `Sfs` values of the phase.

$$
S_{reactive} = S + \sum_i z_i {S_{f,i}}
$$

# Returns

S_reactive

: Entropy of the phase on a reactive basis, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_reactive
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Gibbs free energy of the phase
on a reactive basis.

$$
G_{reactive} = H_{reactive} - TS_{reactive}
$$

# Returns

G_reactive

: Gibbs free energy of the phase on a reactive basis, \[J/(mol)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U_reactive
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the internal energy of the phase
on a reactive basis.

$$
U_{reactive} = H_{reactive} - PV
$$

# Returns

U_reactive

: Internal energy of the phase on a reactive basis, \[J/(mol)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A_reactive
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Helmholtz free energy of the
phase on a reactive basis.

$$
A_{reactive} = U_{reactive} - TS_{reactive}
$$

# Returns

A_reactive

: Helmholtz free energy of the phase on a reactive basis, \[J/(mol)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_formation_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas enthalpy of formation
of the phase (as if the phase was an ideal gas).

$$
H_{formation}^{ig} = \sum_i z_i {H_{f,i}}
$$

# Returns

H_formation_ideal_gas

: Enthalpy of formation of the phase on a formation basis
  as an ideal gas, \[J/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_formation_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas entropy of formation
of the phase (as if the phase was an ideal gas).

$$
S_{formation}^{ig} = \sum_i z_i {S_{f,i}}
$$

# Returns

S_formation_ideal_gas

: Entropy of formation of the phase on a formation basis
  as an ideal gas, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_formation_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas Gibbs free energy of
formation of the phase (as if the phase was an ideal gas).

$$
G_{formation}^{ig} = H_{formation}^{ig} - T_{ref}^{ig} S_{formation}^{ig}
$$

# Returns

G_formation_ideal_gas

: Gibbs free energy of formation of the phase on a formation basis
  as an ideal gas, \[J/(mol)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U_formation_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas internal energy of
formation of the phase (as if the phase was an ideal gas).

$$
U_{formation}^{ig} = H_{formation}^{ig} - P_{ref}^{ig} V^{ig}
$$

# Returns

U_formation_ideal_gas

: Internal energy of formation of the phase on a formation basis
  as an ideal gas, \[J/(mol)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A_formation_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas Helmholtz energy of
formation of the phase (as if the phase was an ideal gas).

$$
A_{formation}^{ig} = U_{formation}^{ig} - T_{ref}^{ig} S_{formation}^{ig}
$$

# Returns

A_formation_ideal_gas

: Helmholtz energy of formation of the phase on a formation basis
  as an ideal gas, \[J/(mol)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_formation_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass ideal-gas formation enthalpy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
H_formation_ideal_gas_mass  : float
    Formation mass enthalpy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_formation_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass ideal-gas formation entropy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
S_formation_ideal_gas_mass  : float
    Formation mass entropy, [J/(kg*K)]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_formation_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass ideal-gas formation Gibbs free energy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
G_formation_ideal_gas_mass  : float
    Formation mass Gibbs free energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U_formation_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas formation mass internal energy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
U_formation_ideal_gas_mass  : float
    Formation mass internal energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A_formation_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas formation mass Helmholtz energy of
the phase.

Returns
-------
A_formation_ideal_gas_mass  : float
    Formation mass Helmholtz energy, [J/kg]

Notes
-----

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cv
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume heat
capacity `Cv` of the phase.

$$
C_v = T\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V^2/ \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T + Cp
$$

# Returns

Cv

: Constant volume molar heat capacity, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dCv_dT_P
documentation:
Method to calculate the temperature derivative of Cv, constant
volume heat capacity, at constant pressure.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial C_v}{\partial T}\right)_P = - \frac{T \operatorname{dPdT_{V}}^{2}{\left(T \right)} \frac{d}{dT} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)}}{\operatorname{dPdV_{T}}^{2} {\left(T \right)}} + \frac{2 T \operatorname{dPdT_{V}}{\left(T \right)} \frac{d}{d T} \operatorname{dPdT_{V}}{\left(T \right)}} {\operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)}} + \frac{\operatorname{ dPdT_{V}}^{2}{\left(T \right)}}{\operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)}} + \frac{d}{d T} \operatorname{Cp}{\left(T \right)}
$$

# Returns

dCv_dT_P

: Temperature derivative of constant volume heat capacity at constant
  pressure, \[J/mol/K^2\]

# Notes

Requires `d2P_dT2_PV`, `d2P_dVdT_TP`, and `d2H_dT2`.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dCv_dP_T
documentation:
Method to calculate the pressure derivative of Cv, constant
volume heat capacity, at constant temperature.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial C_v}{\partial P}\right)_T = - T \operatorname{dPdT_{V}}{\left(P \right)} \frac{d}{d P} \operatorname{dVdT_{P}}{\left(P \right)} - T \operatorname{ dVdT_{P}}{\left(P \right)} \frac{d}{d P} \operatorname{dPdT_{V}} {\left(P \right)} + \frac{d}{d P} \operatorname{Cp}{\left(P\right)}
$$

# Returns

dCv_dP_T

: Pressure derivative of constant volume heat capacity at constant
  temperature, \[J/mol/K/Pa\]

# Notes

Requires `d2V_dTdP`, `d2P_dTdP`, and `d2H_dTdP`.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.chemical_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the chemical potentials of each
component in the phase \[-\]. For a pure substance, this is the
molar Gibbs energy on a reactive basis.

$$
\frac{\partial G}{\partial n_i}_{T, P, N_{j \ne i}}
$$

# Returns

chemical_potential

: Chemical potentials, \[J/mol\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.activities
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the activities of each component
in the phase \[-\].

$$
a_i(T, P, x; f_i^0) = \frac{f_i(T, P, x)}{f_i^0(T, P_i^0)}
$$

# Returns

activities

: Activities, \[-\]
# CORRECT DO NOT CHANGE
# TODO analytical implementation
def to_diff(ns):
tot = sum(ns)
zs = normalize(ns)
return tot*self.to_TP_zs(self.T, self.P, zs).G_reactive()
return jacobian(to_diff, self.zs)

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.gammas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the activity coefficients of the
phase, \[-\].

Activity coefficients are defined as the ratio of
the actual fugacity coefficients times the pressure to the reference
pure fugacity coefficients times the reference pressure.
The reference pressure can be set to the actual pressure (the Lewis
Randall standard state) which makes the pressures cancel.

$$
\gamma_i(T, P, x; f_i^0(T, P_i^0)) = \frac{\phi_i(T, P, x)P}{\phi_i^0(T, P_i^0) P_i^0}
$$

# Returns

gammas

: Activity coefficients, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.gammas_infinite_dilution
documentation:
Calculate and return the infinite dilution activity coefficients
of each component.

Returns
-------
gammas_infinite : list[float]
    Infinite dilution activity coefficients, [-]

Notes
-----
The algorithm is as follows. For each component, set its composition to
zero. Normalize the remaining compositions to 1. Create a new object
with that composition, and calculate the activity coefficient of the
component whose concentration was set to zero.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cp_Cv_ratio
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Cp/Cv ratio of the phase.

$$
\frac{C_p}{C_v}
$$

# Returns

Cp_Cv_ratio

: Cp/Cv ratio, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.isentropic_exponent_PV
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the real gas isentropic exponent
of the phase, which satisfies the relationship
$PV^k = \text{const}$.

$$
k = -\frac{V}{P}\frac{C_p}{C_v}\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T
$$

# Returns

k_PV

: Isentropic exponent of a real fluid, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.isentropic_exponent_PT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the real gas isentropic exponent
of the phase, which satisfies the relationship
$P^{(1-k)}T^k = \text{const}$.

$$
k = \frac{1}{1 - \frac{P}{C_p}\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P}
$$

# Returns

k_PT

: Isentropic exponent of a real fluid, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.isentropic_exponent_TV
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the real gas isentropic exponent
of the phase, which satisfies the relationship
$TV^{k-1} = \text{const}$.

$$
k = 1 + \frac{V}{C_v} \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V
$$

# Returns

k_TV

: Isentropic exponent of a real fluid, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Prandtl
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Prandtl number of the phase

$$
Pr = \frac{C_p \mu}{k} = \frac{\nu}{\alpha} = \frac{C_p \rho \nu}{k}
$$

# Returns

Pr

: Prandtl number \[\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Z
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the compressibility factor of the
phase.

$$
Z = \frac{PV}{RT}
$$

# Returns

Z

: Compressibility factor, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.rho
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar density of the
phase.

$$
\rho = frac{1}{V}
$$

# Returns

rho

: Molar density, \[mol/m^3\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dT_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume pressure
derivative of temperature of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial P}\right)_V = \frac{1}{\left(\frac{ \partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V}
$$

# Returns

dT_dP

: Constant-volume pressure derivative of temperature, \[K/Pa\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dV_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure temperature
derivative of volume of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P = \frac{-\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V} {\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T}
$$

# Returns

dV_dT

: Constant-pressure temperature derivative of volume, \[m^3/(mol\*K)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dV_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature pressure
derivative of volume of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P}\right)_T = {-\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P} {\left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial P}\right)_V}
$$

# Returns

dV_dP

: Constant-temperature pressure derivative of volume, \[m^3/(mol\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dT_dV
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure volume
derivative of temperature of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial V}\right)_P = \frac{1} {\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P}
$$

# Returns

dT_dV

: Constant-pressure volume derivative of temperature, \[K\*m^3/(m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2V_dP2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-temperature pressure
derivative of volume of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial P^2}\right)_T = -\frac{\left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V^2}\right)_T} {\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T^3}
$$

# Returns

d2V_dP2

: Constant-temperature pressure derivative of volume, \[m^3/(mol\*Pa^2)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2T_dP2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-volume second pressure
derivative of temperature of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial P^2}\right)_V = -\left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T^2}\right)_V \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial P}\right)_V^3
$$

# Returns

d2T_dP2

: Constant-volume second pressure derivative of temperature, \[K/Pa^2\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2T_dV2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure second volume
derivative of temperature of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial V^2}\right)_P = -\left[ \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V^2}\right)_T \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V - \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T \partial V}\right) \right] \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)^{-2}_V + \left[\left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T\partial V}\right) \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V - \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T^2}\right)_V\right] \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V^{-3} \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T
$$

# Returns

d2T_dV2

: Constant-pressure second volume derivative of temperature,
  \[K\*mol^2/m^6\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2V_dT2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the constant-pressure second
temperature derivative of volume of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial T^2}\right)_P = -\left[ \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T^2}\right)_V \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T - \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T \partial V}\right) \right] \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)^{-2}_T + \left[\left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T\partial V}\right) \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T - \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V^2}\right)_T\right] \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T^{-3} \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V
$$

# Returns

d2V_dT2

: Constant-pressure second temperature derivative of volume,
  \[m^3/(mol\*K^2)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2V_dPdT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the derivative of pressure and then
the derivative of temperature of volume of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial T\partial P}\right) = - \left[\left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T \partial V}\right) \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T - \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V^2}\right)_T \right]\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T^{-3}
$$

# Returns

d2V_dPdT

: Derivative of pressure and then the derivative of temperature
  of volume, \[m^3/(mol\*K\*Pa)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2T_dPdV
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the derivative of pressure and then
the derivative of volume of temperature of the phase.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial P\partial V}\right) =  - \left[\left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T \partial V}\right)  \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V  - \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T  \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T^2}\right)_V  \right]\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial T}\right)_V^{-3}
$$

# Returns

d2T_dPdV

: Derivative of pressure and then the derivative of volume
  of temperature, \[K\*mol/(Pa\*m^3)\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2P_dVdT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the second derivative of
pressure with respect to temperature and volume of the phase.
This is an alias of `d2P_dTdV`.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V \partial T}
$$

# Returns

d2P_dVdT

: Second volume derivative of pressure, \[mol\*Pa^2/(J\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dZ_dzs
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole fraction derivatives of the
compressibility factor `Z` of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial Z}{\partial z_i}
$$

# Returns

dZ_dzs

: Mole fraction derivatives of the compressibility factor of the
  phase, \[-\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dZ_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole number derivatives of the
compressibility factor `Z` of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial Z}{\partial n_i}
$$

# Returns

dZ_dns

: Mole number derivatives of the compressibility factor of the
  phase, \[1/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dV_dzs
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole fraction derivatives of the
molar volume `V` of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial V}{\partial z_i}
$$

# Returns

dV_dzs

: Mole fraction derivatives of the molar volume of the phase, \[m^3/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dV_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mole number derivatives of the
molar volume `V` of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial V}{\partial n_i}
$$

# Returns

dV_dns

: Mole number derivatives of the molar volume of the phase, \[m^3/mol^2\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dnV_dns
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the partial mole number derivatives
of the molar volume `V` of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial n V}{\partial n_i}
$$

# Returns

dnV_dns

: Partial mole number derivatives of the molar volume of the phase,
  \[m^3/mol\]

# Notes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.PIP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the phase identification parameter
of the phase.

$$
\Pi = V \left[\frac{\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V \partial T}} {\frac{\partial P }{\partial T}}- \frac{\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V^2}}{\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}} \right]
$$

# Returns

PIP

: Phase identification parameter, \[-\]
Derived properties

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.kappa
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the isothermal compressibility
of the phase.

$$
\kappa = -\frac{1}{V}\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P} \right)_T
$$

# Returns

kappa

: Isothermal coefficient of compressibility, \[1/Pa\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dkappa_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of
isothermal compressibility of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \kappa}{\partial T} = -\frac{ \left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial P\partial T} \right)}{V} + \frac{\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P} \right)_T\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T} \right)_P}{V^2}
$$

# Returns

dkappa_dT

: First temperature derivative of isothermal coefficient of
  compressibility, \[1/(Pa\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.isothermal_bulk_modulus
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the isothermal bulk modulus
of the phase.

$$
K_T = -V\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V} \right)_T
$$

# Returns

isothermal_bulk_modulus

: Isothermal bulk modulus, \[Pa\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.isobaric_expansion
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the isobatic expansion coefficient
of the phase.

$$
\beta = \frac{1}{V}\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T} \right)_P
$$

# Returns

beta

: Isobaric coefficient of a thermal expansion, \[1/K\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.disobaric_expansion_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of
isobatic expansion coefficient of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \beta}{\partial T} = \frac{1}{V}\left( \left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial T^2} \right)_P - \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T} \right)_P^2/V \right)
$$

# Returns

dbeta_dT

: Temperature derivative of isobaric coefficient of a thermal
  expansion, \[1/K^2\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.disobaric_expansion_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative of
isobatic expansion coefficient of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \beta}{\partial P} = \frac{1}{V}\left( \left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial T\partial P} \right) -\frac{ \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T} \right)_P  \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P} \right)_T}{V} \right)
$$

# Returns

dbeta_dP

: Pressure derivative of isobaric coefficient of a thermal
  expansion, \[1/(K\*Pa)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Joule_Thomson
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the Joule-Thomson coefficient
of the phase.

$$
\mu_{JT} = \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial P}\right)_H = \frac{1}{C_p} \left[T \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P - V\right] = \frac{V}{C_p}\left(\beta T-1\right)
$$

# Returns

mu_JT

: Joule-Thomson coefficient \[K/Pa\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.speed_of_sound
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar speed of sound
of the phase.

$$
w = \left[-V^2 \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T \frac{C_p} {C_v}\right]^{1/2}
$$

A similar expression based on molar density is:

$$
w = \left[\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial \rho}\right)_T \frac{C_p} {C_v}\right]^{1/2}
$$

# Returns

w

: Speed of sound for a real gas, \[m\*kg^0.5/(s\*mol^0.5)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.speed_of_sound_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the mass speed of sound
of an ideal gas phase at the current conditions.

$$
c = \sqrt{kR_{specific, ideal gas}T}
$$

# Returns

w

: Speed of sound for an ideal gas, \[m/s\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.speed_of_sound_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar speed of sound
of an ideal gas phase at the current conditions.

$$
w = \left[-V^2 \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T \frac{C_p} {C_v}\right]^{1/2}
$$

$$
\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T = \frac{-P^2}{RT}
$$

# Returns

w

: Speed of sound for a real gas, \[m\*kg^0.5/(s\*mol^0.5)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dZ_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of
compressibility of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial Z}{\partial P} = P\frac{\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P - \frac{-V}{T}}{RT}
$$

# Returns

dZ_dT

: Temperature derivative of compressibility, \[1/K\]
## Compressibility factor derivatives

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dZ_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative of
compressibility of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial Z}{\partial P} = \frac{V + P\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P}\right)_T}{RT}
$$

# Returns

dZ_dP

: Pressure derivative of compressibility, \[1/Pa\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dZ_dV
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the volume derivative of
compressibility of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial Z}{\partial V} = \frac{P - \rho \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial \rho}\right)_T}{RT}
$$

# Returns

dZ_dV

: Volume derivative of compressibility, \[mol/(m^3)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Bvirial
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the `B` virial coefficient of the
phase at its current conditions.

Returns
-------
Bvirial : float
    Virial coefficient, [m^3/mol]

Notes
-----
Could add more

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dP_drho
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar density derivative of
pressure of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial P}{\partial \rho} = -V^2\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T
$$

# Returns

dP_drho

: Molar density derivative of pressure, \[Pa\*m^3/mol\]
## Derivatives in the molar density basis

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.drho_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative of
molar density of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial P} = -\frac{1}{V^2}\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P}\right)_T
$$

# Returns

drho_dP

: Pressure derivative of Molar density, \[mol/(Pa\*m^3)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2P_drho2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the second molar density derivative
of pressure of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial \rho^2} = -V^2\left( -V^2 \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial V^2}\right)_T -2 V \left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V}\right)_T \right)
$$

# Returns

d2P_drho2

: Second molar density derivative of pressure, \[Pa\*m^6/mol^2\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2rho_dP2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the second pressure derivative
of molar density of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 \rho}{\partial P^2} = -\frac{1}{V^2} \left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial P^2}\right)_T + \frac{2}{V^3} \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P}\right)_T^2
$$

# Returns

d2rho_dP2

: Second pressure derivative of molar density, \[mol^2/(Pa\*m^6)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dT_drho
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the molar density derivative of
temperature of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial T}{\partial \rho} = -V^2\left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial V}\right)_P
$$

# Returns

dT_drho

: Molar density derivative of temperature, \[K\*m^3/mol\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2T_drho2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the second molar density derivative
of temperature of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial \rho^2} = -V^2\left( -V^2 \left(\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial V^2}\right)_P -2 V \left(\frac{\partial T}{\partial V}\right)_P \right)
$$

# Returns

d2T_drho2

: Second molar density derivative of temperature, \[K\*m^6/mol^2\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.drho_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of
molar density of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial T} = -\frac{1}{V^2}\left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P
$$

# Returns

drho_dT

: Temperature derivative of molar density, \[mol/(K\*m^3)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2rho_dT2
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the second temperature derivative
of molar density of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 \rho}{\partial T^2} = -\frac{1}{V^2} \left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial T^2}\right)_P + \frac{2}{V^3} \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_T^2
$$

# Returns

d2rho_dT2

: Second temperature derivative of molar density, \[mol^2/(K\*m^6)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2P_dTdrho
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative
and then molar density derivative of the pressure of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T \partial \rho} = -V^2 \left(\frac{\partial^2 P}{\partial T \partial V}\right)
$$

# Returns

d2P_dTdrho

: Temperature derivative and then molar density derivative of the
  pressure, \[Pa\*m^3/(K\*mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2T_dPdrho
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative
and then molar density derivative of the temperature of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial P \partial \rho} = -V^2 \left(\frac{\partial^2 T}{\partial P \partial V}\right)
$$

# Returns

d2T_dPdrho

: Pressure derivative and then molar density derivative of the
  temperature, \[K\*m^3/(Pa\*mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.d2rho_dPdT
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the pressure derivative
and then temperature derivative of the molar density of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial^2 \rho}{\partial P \partial T} = -\frac{1}{V^2} \left(\frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial P \partial T}\right) + \frac{2}{V^3}  \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial T}\right)_P  \left(\frac{\partial V}{\partial P}\right)_T
$$

# Returns

d2rho_dPdT

: Pressure derivative and then temperature derivative of the
  molar density, \[mol/(m^3\*K\*Pa)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.drho_dV_T
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the volume derivative of
molar density of the phase.

$$
\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial V} = -\frac{1}{V^2}
$$

# Returns

drho_dV_T

: Molar density derivative of volume, \[mol^2/m^6\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.drho_dT_V
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the temperature derivative of
molar density of the phase at constant volume.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial T}\right)_V = 0
$$

# Returns

drho_dT_V

: Temperature derivative of molar density of the phase at constant
  volume, \[mol/(m^3\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase._setup_Cpigs
documentation: Idea gas heat capacity

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cpigs_pure
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas heat capacities of
every component in the phase. This method is powered by the
`HeatCapacityGases` objects, except when all components have the same
heat capacity form and a fast implementation has been written for it
(currently only polynomials).

Returns
-------
Cp_ig : list[float]
    Molar ideal gas heat capacities, [J/(mol*K)]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cpig_integrals_pure
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the integrals of the ideal-gas heat
capacities of every component in the phase from a temperature of
{obj}`Phase.T_REF_IG` to the system temperature. This method is powered by the
`HeatCapacityGases` objects, except when all components have the same
heat capacity form and a fast implementation has been written for it
(currently only polynomials).

$$
\Delta H^{ig} = \int^T_{T_{ref}} C_p^{ig} dT
$$

# Returns

dH_ig

: Integrals of ideal gas heat capacity from the reference
  temperature to the system temperature, \[J/(mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cpig_integrals_over_T_pure
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the integrals of the ideal-gas heat
capacities divided by temperature of every component in the phase from
a temperature of {obj}`Phase.T_REF_IG` to the system temperature.
This method is powered by the
`HeatCapacityGases` objects, except when all components have the same
heat capacity form and a fast implementation has been written for it
(currently only polynomials).

$$
\Delta S^{ig} = \int^T_{T_{ref}} \frac{C_p^{ig}}{T} dT
$$

# Returns

dS_ig

: Integrals of ideal gas heat capacity over temperature from the
  reference temperature to the system temperature, \[J/(mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dCpigs_dT_pure
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the first temperature derivative of
ideal-gas heat capacities of every component in the phase. This method
is powered by the `HeatCapacityGases` objects, except when all
components have the same heat capacity form and a fast implementation
has been written for it (currently only polynomials).

$$
\frac{\partial C_p^{ig}}{\partial T}
$$

# Returns

dCp_ig_dT

: First temperature derivatives of molar ideal gas heat capacities,
  \[J/(mol\*K^2)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.V_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas molar volume of the
phase.

$$
V^{ig} = \frac{RT}{P}
$$

# Returns

V

: Ideal gas molar volume, \[m^3/mol\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.H_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas enthalpy of the phase.

$$
H^{ig} = \sum_i z_i {H_{i}^{ig}}
$$

# Returns

H

: Ideal gas enthalpy, \[J/(mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.S_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas entropy of the phase.

$$
S^{ig} = \sum_i z_i S_{i}^{ig} - R\ln\left(\frac{P}{P_{ref}}\right) - R\sum_i z_i \ln(z_i)
$$

# Returns

S

: Ideal gas molar entropy, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cp_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas heat capacity of the
phase.

$$
C_p^{ig} = \sum_i z_i {C_{p,i}^{ig}}
$$

# Returns

Cp

: Ideal gas heat capacity, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cp_ideal_gas_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return mass constant pressure
departure heat capacity of the phase.

$$
Cp_{ideal, mass} = \frac{1000 Cp_{ideal, molar}}{MW}
$$

# Returns

Cp_ideal_gas_mass

: Mass departure heat capacity, \[J/(kg\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cv_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas constant volume heat
capacity of the phase.

$$
C_v^{ig} = \sum_i z_i {C_{p,i}^{ig}} - R
$$

# Returns

Cv

: Ideal gas constant volume heat capacity, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cv_dep
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the difference between the actual
`Cv` and the ideal-gas constant volume heat
capacity $C_v^{ig}$ of the phase.

$$
C_v^{dep} = C_v - C_v^{ig}
$$

# Returns

Cv_dep

: Departure ideal gas constant volume heat capacity, \[J/(mol\*K)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.Cp_Cv_ratio_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ratio of the ideal-gas heat
capacity to its constant-volume heat capacity.

$$
\frac{C_p^{ig}}{C_v^{ig}}
$$

# Returns

Cp_Cv_ratio_ideal_gas

: Cp/Cv for the phase as an ideal gas, \[-\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.G_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas Gibbs free energy of
the phase.

$$
G^{ig} = H^{ig} - T S^{ig}

# Returns

G_ideal_gas

: Ideal gas free energy, \[J/(mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.U_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas internal energy of
the phase.

$$
U^{ig} = H^{ig} - P V^{ig}

U_ideal_gas

: Ideal gas internal energy, \[J/(mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.A_ideal_gas
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the ideal-gas Helmholtz energy of
the phase.

$$
A^{ig} = U^{ig} - T S^{ig}
$$

# Returns

A_ideal_gas

: Ideal gas Helmholtz free energy, \[J/(mol)\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase._derivs_jacobian
documentation:
Calculates and returns a first-order derivative of one property
with respect to another property at constant another property.

This is particularly useful to obtain derivatives with respect to
another property which is not an intensive variable in a model,
allowing for example derivatives at constant enthalpy or Gibbs energy
to be obtained. This formula is obtained from the first derivative
principles of reciprocity, the chain rule, and the triple product rule
as shown in [^footnote-1].

... math::

: left(frac{partial a}{partial b}right)\_\{c}=
  frac{left(frac{partial a}{partial x}right)\_\{y}left(
  frac{partial c}{partial y}right)\_\{x}-left(frac{partial a}{
  partial y}right)\_\{x}left(frac{partial c}{partial x}
  right)\_\{y}}{left(frac{partial b}{partial x}right)\_\{y}left(
  frac{partial c}{partial y}right)\_\{x}-left(frac{partial b}
  {partial y}right)\_\{x}left(frac{partial c}{partial x}
  right)\_\{y}}

# References

[^footnote-1]: Thorade, Matthis, and Ali Saadat. "Partial Derivatives of
    Thermodynamic State Properties for Dynamic Simulation."
    Environmental Earth Sciences 70, no. 8 (April 10, 2013): 3497-3503.
    <https://doi.org/10.1007/s12665-013-2394-z>.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.MW
documentation:
Method to calculate and return molecular weight of the phase.

$$
\text{MW} = \sum_i z_i \text{MW}_i
$$

# Returns

MW

: Molecular weight, \[g/mol\]
## Transport properties - pass them on!
Properties that use `constants` attributes

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.MW_inv
documentation:
Method to calculate and return inverse of molecular weight of the
phase.

$$
\frac{1}{\text{MW}} = \frac{1}{\sum_i z_i \text{MW}_i}
$$

# Returns

MW_inv

: Inverse of molecular weight, \[mol/g\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.speed_of_sound_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return the speed of sound
of the phase.

$$
w = \left[-V^2 \frac{1000}{MW}\left(\frac{\partial P}{\partial V} \right)_T \frac{C_p}{C_v}\right]^{1/2}
$$

# Returns

w

: Speed of sound for a real gas, \[m/s\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.rho_mass
documentation:
Method to calculate and return mass density of the phase.

$$
\rho = \frac{MW}{1000\cdot VM}
$$

# Returns

rho_mass

: Mass density, \[kg/m^3\]

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.drho_mass_dT
documentation:
Method to calculate the mass density derivative with respect to
temperature, at constant pressure.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial T}\right)_{P} = \frac{-\text{MW} \frac{\partial V_m}{\partial T}}{1000 V_m^2}
$$

# Returns

drho_mass_dT

: Temperature derivative of mass density at constant pressure,
  \[kg/m^3/K\]

# Notes

Requires `dV_dT`, `MW`, and `V`.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.drho_mass_dP
documentation:
Method to calculate the mass density derivative with respect to
pressure, at constant temperature.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial \rho}{\partial P}\right)_{T} = \frac{-\text{MW} \frac{\partial V_m}{\partial P}}{1000 V_m^2}
$$

# Returns

drho_mass_dP

: Pressure derivative of mass density at constant temperature,
  \[kg/m^3/Pa\]

# Notes

Requires `dV_dP`, `MW`, and `V`.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.P_max_at_V
documentation:
Dummy method. The idea behind this method, which is implemented by some

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dspeed_of_sound_dT_P
documentation:
Method to calculate the temperature derivative of speed of sound
at constant pressure in molar units.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial c}{\partial T}\right)_P = - \frac{\sqrt{- \frac{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(T \right)} V^{2} {\left(T \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)}} {\operatorname{Cv}{\left(T \right)}}} \left(- \frac{\operatorname{Cp} {\left(T \right)} V^{2}{\left(T \right)} \frac{d}{d T} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)}}{2 \operatorname{Cv}{\left(T \right)}} - \frac{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(T \right)} V{\left(T \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)} \frac{d}{d T} V{\left(T \right)}}{\operatorname{Cv}{\left(T \right)}} + \frac{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(T \right)} V^{2}{\left(T \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)} \frac{d}{d T} \operatorname{Cv}{\left(T \right)}}{2 \operatorname{Cv}^{2} {\left(T \right)}} - \frac{V^{2}{\left(T \right)} \operatorname{ dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)} \frac{d}{d T} \operatorname{Cp}{\left(T \right)}}{2 \operatorname{Cv}{\left(T \right)}}\right) \operatorname{Cv}{\left(T \right)}}{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(T \right)} V^{2}{\left(T \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(T \right)}}
$$

# Returns

dspeed_of_sound_dT_P

: Temperature derivative of speed of sound at constant pressure,
  \[m\*kg^0.5/s/mol^0.5/K\]

# Notes

Requires the temperature derivative of Cp and Cv both at constant
pressure, as wel as the volume and temperature derivative of pressure,
calculated at constant temperature and then pressure respectively.
These can be tricky to obtain.

methods.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.dspeed_of_sound_dP_T
documentation:
Method to calculate the pressure derivative of speed of sound
at constant temperature in molar units.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial c}{\partial P}\right)_T = - \frac{\sqrt{- \frac{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(P \right)} V^{2} {\left(P \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(P \right)}} {\operatorname{Cv}{\left(P \right)}}} \left(- \frac{ \operatorname{Cp}{\left(P \right)} V^{2}{\left(P \right)} \frac{d} {d P} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(P \right)}}{2 \operatorname{Cv} {\left(P \right)}} - \frac{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(P \right)} V{\left(P \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(P \right)} \frac{d}{d P} V{\left(P \right)}}{\operatorname{Cv}{\left(P \right) }} + \frac{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(P \right)} V^{2}{\left(P \right) } \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(P \right)} \frac{d}{d P} \operatorname{Cv}{\left(P \right)}}{2 \operatorname{Cv}^{2}{\left(P \right)}} - \frac{V^{2}{\left(P \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}} {\left(P \right)} \frac{d}{d P} \operatorname{Cp}{\left(P \right)}} {2 \operatorname{Cv}{\left(P \right)}}\right) \operatorname{Cv} {\left(P \right)}}{\operatorname{Cp}{\left(P \right)} V^{2}{\left(P \right)} \operatorname{dPdV_{T}}{\left(P \right)}}
$$

# Returns

dspeed_of_sound_dP_T

: Pressure derivative of speed of sound at constant temperature,
  \[m\*kg^0.5/s/mol^0.5/Pa\]

# Notes

class_variables.name: thermo.phases.phase.Phase.reference_pointer_dicts
documentation: Tuple of dictionaries for object -> string

`Phase` is the base class for all phase objects in `thermo`. Each
sub-class implements a number of core properties; many other properties
can be calculated from them.

Among those properties are `H`, `S`, `Cp`, `dP_dT`, `dP_dV`,
`d2P_dT2`, `d2P_dV2`, and `d2P_dTdV`.

An additional set of properties that can be implemented and that enable
more functionality are `dH_dP`, `dS_dT`, `dS_dP`, `d2H_dT2`, `d2H_dP2`,
`d2S_dP2`, `dH_dT_V`, `dH_dP_V`, `dH_dV_T`, `dH_dV_P`, `dS_dT_V`,
`dS_dP_V`, `d2H_dTdP`, `d2H_dT2_V`, `d2P_dTdP`, `d2P_dVdP`, `d2P_dVdT_TP`,
`d2P_dT2_PV`.

Some models may re-implement properties which would normally be
calculated by this `Phase` base class because they have more explicit,
faster ways of calculating the property.

When a phase object is the result of a Flash calculation, the resulting
phase objects have a reference to a
{obj}`ChemicalConstantsPackage <thermo.chemical_package.ChemicalConstantsPackage>`
object and all of its properties can be accessed from from the resulting
phase objects as well.

A {obj}`ChemicalConstantsPackage <thermo.chemical_package.ChemicalConstantsPackage>`
object can also be manually set to the attribute `constants` to enable
access to those properties. This includes mass-based properties, which are
not accessible from Phase objects without a reference to the constants.

$$
\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_V = \frac{C_p^{ig}}{T} - \frac{R}{P}\frac{\partial P}{\partial T} + \left(\frac{\partial S_{dep}}{\partial T}\right)_V
$$

# Returns

dS_dT_V

: First temperature derivative of molar entropy at constant volume,
  \[J/(mol\*K^2)\]

functions.name: thermo.regular_solution.regular_solution_gammas_binaries
documentation:
Calculates activity coefficients with the regular solution model
at fixed `lambda` values for
a binary system at a series of mole fractions at specified temperatures.
This is used for
regression of `lambda` parameters. This function is highly optimized,
and operates on multiple points at a time.

$$
\ln \gamma_1 = \frac{V_1\phi_2^2}{RT}\left[     (\text{SP}_1-\text{SP}_2)^2 + \lambda_{12}\text{SP}_1\text{SP}_2     + \lambda_{21}\text{SP}_1\text{SP}_2     \right]
$$

$$
\ln \gamma_2 =  \frac{V_2\phi_1^2}{RT}\left[     (\text{SP}_1-\text{SP}_2)^2 + \lambda_{12}\text{SP}_1\text{SP}_2     + \lambda_{21}\text{SP}_1\text{SP}_2     \right]
$$

$$
\phi_1 = \frac{x_1 V_1}{x_1 V_1 + x_2 V_2}
$$

$$
\phi_2 = \frac{x_2 V_2}{x_1 V_1 + x_2 V_2}
$$

# Parameters

xs

: Liquid mole fractions of each species in the format
  x0_0, x1_0, (component 1 point1, component 2 point 1),
  x0_1, x1_1, (component 1 point2, component 2 point 2), ...
  size pts\*2
  \[-\]

Vs

: Molar volumes of each of the two components, \[m^3/mol\]

SPs

: Solubility parameters of each of the two components, \[Pa^0.5\]

Ts

: Temperatures of each composition point; half the length of `xs`, \[K\]

lambda12

: `lambda` parameter for 12, \[-\]

lambda21

: `lambda` parameter for 21, \[-\]

gammas

: Array to store the activity coefficient for each species in the liquid
  mixture, indexed the same as `xs`; can be omitted or provided
  for slightly better performance \[-\]

# Returns

gammas

: Activity coefficient for each species in the liquid mixture,
  indexed the same as `xs`, \[-\]

# Notes

# Examples

```pycon
>>> regular_solution_gammas_binaries([.1, .9, 0.3, 0.7, .85, .15], Vs=[7.421e-05, 8.068e-05], SPs=[19570.2, 18864.7], Ts=[300.0, 400.0, 500.0], lambda12=0.1759, lambda21=0.7991)
[6818.90697, 1.105437, 62.6628, 2.01184, 1.181434, 137.6232]
```
MIN_LAMBDA_REGULAR_SOLUTION = -10.0
MAX_LAMBDA_REGULAR_SOLUTION = 10.0

functions.name: thermo.regular_solution.hansen_Aijs_His
documentation:
Calculate either Aijs or His matrix for Hansen model using specified multiplier.
Supports `alpha` as a scaling factor.

Parameters
----------
delta_d : list[float]
    Hansen dispersive parameters for each component, [Pa^0.5]
delta_p : list[float]
    Hansen polar parameters for each component, [Pa^0.5]
delta_h : list[float]
    Hansen hydrogen bonding parameters for each component, [Pa^0.5]
output : list[list[float]], optional
    Pre-allocated symmetric matrix to store results
alpha : float, optional
    Optional scaling factor, defaults to 1.0, [-]
multiplier : float
    Multiplier to convert between Aijs (0.5) and His (1.0) calculations, [-]
    
Returns
-------
output : list[list[float]]
    Calculated symmetric matrix of interaction parameters, [Pa]

Notes
-----
The calculations produce a symmetric matrix where each element represents the
interaction energy between components i and j. The total interaction energy
includes dispersive, polar, and hydrogen bonding contributions, with the polar
and hydrogen bonding terms weighted by 0.25 to account for their relative
contributions to the total cohesive energy.
